title: encephalartos villosus f. hildebrandtii
intro: Encephalartos villosus f. hildebrandtii, commonly known as the Hildebrandt's cycad, is a species of plant in the family Zamiaceae. It is a cycad, a type of ancient plant that has been in existence for millions of years. The health benefits of Encephalartos villosus f. hildebrandtii include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, while its therapeutic actions include expectorant and bronchodilator properties. The bioactive constituents of the plant include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

side_effects: Encephalartos villosus f. hildebrandtii can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea and nausea, due to its high saponin content. Consuming excessive amounts can also lead to allergic reactions, ranging from mild skin irritation to anaphylaxis in severe cases. Furthermore, using the plant for medicinal purposes without proper guidance can cause kidney damage and interact with other medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions.
